name: OOD: French translation pipeline
description: Plan-to-commands evaluation captured from production log on 2026-04-30 at 16:07:37.
user_prompt:
Convert this workflow plan to JSON commands:

Workflow plan: French email translation batch

Important deployment note: There is no trigger node in this platform. To make this workflow "watch" C:\incoming\french_emails, build the workflow below and then create a separate platform schedule to run it on a polling interval (for example every 5 minutes). The workflow itself starts with a Folder Selector that scans the folder each run.

1. Add a Folder Selector node.
   - Purpose: Find all .txt files in C:\incoming\french_emails each time the workflow runs.
   - Configuration:
     - folderPath: C:\incoming\french_emails
     - selectionMode: pattern match
     - filePattern: *.txt
     - includeSubfolders: false
     - failIfNoFilesFound: false
     - outputVariable: selectedFiles
   - Outgoing connection: pass -> Step 2

2. Add a Set Variable node.
   - Purpose: Initialize an empty array to hold translation rows for optional reporting/counting and keep the workflow state explicit.
   - Configuration:
     - variables:
       - translationRows = [] (expression mode)
   - Outgoing connection: pass -> Step 3

3. Add a Loop node.
   - Purpose: Iterate through each file path returned by the Folder Selector.
   - Configuration:
     - inputArray: ${selectedFiles}
     - itemVariable: currentFile
     - indexVariable: currentIndex
     - maxIterations: 10000
   - Outgoing connection: loop body -> Step 4
   - After loop completion, continue to Step 10

4. Add a File node inside the loop.
   - Purpose: Read the contents of the current text file.
   - Configuration:
     - operation: read
     - filePath: ${currentFile}
     - saveToVariable: true
     - outputVariable: fileContents
   - Outgoing connection: pass -> Step 5

5. Add a Set Variable node inside the loop.
   - Purpose: Derive a clean filename from the current file path for Excel output.
   - Configuration:
     - variables:
       - currentFileName = currentFile.split('\\')[-1] (expression mode)
   - Outgoing connection: pass -> Step 6

6. Add an AI Action node inside the loop.
   - Purpose: Translate the French email text into English.
   - Configuration:
     - agentId: 372
     - prompt: Translate the following French email into clear, natural English. Preserve the meaning, tone, names, dates, order numbers, and any customer-specific details. Return only the English translation with no commentary or labels.

${fileContents}
     - outputVariable: translatedText
   - Outgoing connection: pass -> Step 7

7. Add a Set Variable node inside the loop.
   - Purpose: Build a single-row object for this translated file and also keep an accumulated in-memory list.
   - Configuration:
     - variables:
       - translationRow = {"file_name": currentFileName, "file_path": currentFile, "translated_text": translatedText} (expression mode)
       - translationRows = translationRows + [translationRow] (expression mode)
   - Outgoing connection: pass -> Step 8

8. Add an Excel Export node inside the loop.
   - Purpose: Append one row per translated file to translations.xlsx.
   - Configuration:
     - dataVariable: ${translationRow}
     - excelOperation: append
     - excelOutputPath: C:\incoming\french_emails\translations.xlsx
     - excelTemplatePath: C:\incoming\french_emails\translations.xlsx
     - column mapping:
       - file_name -> file_name
       - file_path -> file_path
       - translated_text -> translated_text
     - outputVariable: excelAppendResult
   - Outgoing connection: pass -> Step 9

9. Add an End Loop node.
   - Purpose: End the current iteration and return control to the Loop node.
   - Configuration: default
   - Outgoing connection: pass -> Step 3 only (exactly one outgoing pass connection back to the Loop node)

10. Add a Set Variable node after loop completion.
   - Purpose: Build the batch completion email subject and body using simple variables so the Alert node can reference them safely.
   - Configuration:
     - variables:
       - translatedFileCount = len(selectedFiles) (expression mode)
       - batchEmailSubject = f"French email translation batch complete: {translatedFileCount} file(s) processed" (expression mode)
       - batchEmailMessage = f"The French email translation batch has completed. Files processed: {translatedFileCount}. Source folder: C:\\incoming\\french_emails. Output workbook: C:\\incoming\\french_emails\\translations.xlsx." (expression mode)
   - Outgoing connection: pass -> Step 11

11. Add an Alert node.
   - Purpose: Send a completion email to support@acme.com when the batch finishes.
   - Configuration:
     - alertType: Email
     - to: support@acme.com
     - emailSubject: ${batchEmailSubject}
     - messageTemplate: ${batchEmailMessage}
   - Outgoing connection: pass -> end of workflow

Connections summary:
- Step 1 pass -> Step 2
- Step 2 pass -> Step 3
- Step 3 loop body -> Step 4
- Step 4 pass -> Step 5
- Step 5 pass -> Step 6
- Step 6 pass -> Step 7
- Step 7 pass -> Step 8
- Step 8 pass -> Step 9
- Step 9 pass -> Step 3
- Step 3 completed/exit -> Step 10
- Step 10 pass -> Step 11

IDs and explicit details required by builder:
- AI Action must use agent ID 372
- Folder path: C:\incoming\french_emails
- Input file pattern: *.txt
- Excel file path: C:\incoming\french_emails\translations.xlsx
- Notification recipient: support@acme.com
